name: Misfortune
description: Bad Luck, Calamity, Accidents, Failure, Ruin.

control.name: Destined to Fail
tiers: Choose one target — they subtract 1d4 from their next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw before the end of your next turn.
On a failed Wisdom save, the target must reroll their next successful saving throw and take the lower result. This triggers once before the effect ends.
Once per round as a reaction, when a creature within 60 feet succeeds on a saving throw, you may force them to reroll it — they must take the new result.
For 1 minute on a failed save, the target rolls all saving throws with disadvantage. They may repeat the Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of their turns to end the effect early.

control.name: Chain of Calamity
control.description: Bad luck cascades into greater disaster.
tiers: If a creature fails a saving throw against your effect, the nearest creature within 10 feet must make a Dexterity save or suffer 1d6 damage of the same type.
Chain to up to 2 creatures within 30 feet. If the first creature fails their save, each chained creature rolls their next saving throw with disadvantage.

When a creature affected by this effect fails a saving throw, one creature of your choice within 15 feet must make the same saving throw against a DC of 10 or suffer the same condition (not damage) as a ripple of misfortune.

When a creature within 60 feet rolls a critical failure (natural 1 on a save or attack), trigger a Calamity — they are immediately subjected to one additional effect of your choice: Stunned until end of next turn, knocked Prone, or Cursed with disadvantage on their next saving throw.

control.name: Cursed Timing
control.description: The wrong action at the worst time.
tiers: A creature affected has disadvantage on opportunity attacks and reaction-based effects until the end of your next turn.
Their initiative score drops by 5 for the remainder of the encounter, as misfortune robs them of their sense of timing.
On a failed save, the target expends one use of Legendary Resistance if they have any remaining. If they have none, they instead have disadvantage on their next saving throw.
Once per long rest, designate one creature — until the end of their next turn they cannot use bonus actions or reactions, as every opportunity they reach for slips away at the last moment.

control.name: False Fortune
tiers: If a creature rolls a natural 20 on an attack roll, they must reroll it — if the reroll is 10 or lower, the attack misses and backfires in a minor way at DM discretion.
The target's next critical hit becomes a normal hit, and they take psychic damage equal to your spellcasting modifier as their fortune inverts.
Once per long rest as a reaction, you may convert one instance of advantage a creature within 60 feet has into disadvantage before they roll.

Mark a creature with Cursed Glory for 1 minute — if they roll a natural 20 on any roll, they immediately take psychic damage equal to twice your spellcasting modifier, and the natural 20 is treated as a natural 1 instead.

complementEffects.name: Hex Touch
complementEffects.description: The spell leaves a lingering curse where it lands.
complementEffects.effect: On a hit or failed saving throw against the primary effect, the target is hexed — they suffer a −1d4 penalty to one type of roll of your choice (attack rolls, saving throws, or ability checks) until the end of their next turn.
upgrades: Level 5+: The penalty increases to −1d6, and you may apply it to two types of rolls simultaneously.
Level 11+: The hex persists for 1 minute, and once per turn when the hexed creature rolls the affected roll type, they must reroll one die and take the lower result.

complementEffects.name: Deflect Ill Omen
complementEffects.description: The curse finds someone else to land on.
complementEffects.effect: When the primary effect resolves, one ally of your choice within 30 feet gains the Deflection benefit — the next time they would fail a saving throw or ability check before the start of your next turn, they may reroll it once and take the higher result.
upgrades: Level 5+: The ally may apply this reroll to attack rolls as well, and the benefit persists until the start of your next turn.
Level 11+: You may choose two allies to receive this benefit, and each reroll gains a +2 bonus.

complementEffects.name: Stumble
complementEffects.effect: When the primary effect hits or a target fails their saving throw, that target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against your Verum DC or fall prone as misfortune finds the one uneven stone, the one slick patch, the one moment their balance was not quite right.
upgrades: Level 5+: On a failed save, the target also has their movement speed halved until the end of their next turn.
Level 11+: Creatures that fail also have disadvantage on the first attack roll they make after standing up.
